description: The handle of this harvesting knife is made of wood, with a handlebar-shaped iron blade inserted at the middle of the handle to cut rice stalks. The ends of the iron blade have different designs: one is jagged at the tip, and the other is grooved and pointed.
condition: Fair and stable condition, with abrasions on the handle and inactive corrosion on the blade.

images.alt: Harvesting knife (pang-ani) with a mustache-shaped iron blade set through a wooden dowel handle, its two jagged and grooved tips curving outward
The pang-ani's wooden handle end, inked with its accession number, angled to show the iron blade's curve and pointed tip

rights: Images courtesy of the Ethnologisches Museum, Staatliche Museen zu Berlin.
